success_factors: Workflow completed end-to-end with status=success across all expected nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
High import feasibility (0.92) and low intrusiveness risk enabled adapter_mode='import' with minimal repository modification.
MCP plugin booted successfully with healthy service status over stdio transport.
Core repository structure and AST-discovered functions/classes were rich enough to auto-generate a broad endpoint surface.
failure_reasons: Validation quality is limited because original project tests were not executed meaningfully (passed=false, execution_time=0, empty details).
DeepWiki enrichment failed, reducing external/documentation-aware context for generation and review.
Generation metrics appear incomplete/inconsistent (tool_endpoints=0 and LOC/size=0 despite many generated files/endpoints), indicating instrumentation/reporting gaps.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully via zip fallback; timing per node not provided, but repository ingest was reliable.
node_performance.analysis_time: Completed successfully; AST scan identified extensive modules/functions/classes. Medium complexity was handled without blocking.
node_performance.generation_time: Completed successfully; produced MCP scaffold and many endpoints. However, reported generation counters are inconsistent with actual outputs.
node_performance.test_time: MCP health test passed quickly (startup_time=0 reported), but original project test validation is effectively missing, creating a confidence gap.
